mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors seek yield in risk assets.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.05 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 100k/month signals robust labor market demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved profit margins.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 50%+ of periods when PAYEMS exceeds 100k, or earnings revisions fail to rise by >2% in same window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction (HOUST) above 550k units signals improved consumer wealth and spending momentum, creating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through housing-led demand expansion.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 40%+ of periods when HOUST exceeds 550k, or housing starts fail to grow by >5% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) below 93.5 signals weakening manufacturing activity, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through reduced corporate revenue expectations and sector-specific earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in 50%+ of periods when INDPRO falls below 93.5, or PMI manufacturing indices rise by >1.5pp in same window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) above thresholds indicate strong demand-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through sectoral earnings acceleration.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 40%+ of periods when both RSAFS and HOUST exceed thresholds, or demand-side PMI declines by >1.0pp.

mechanism_narrative: A 20bp+ increase in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals central bank policy tightening, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through higher discount rates and reduced earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 50%+ of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5, or real rates fail to rise by >1.0pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment (UNRATE) below 9% and increases in payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 100k signal labor market overheating, creating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through sectoral reflation and wage-driven demand.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 40%+ of periods when both UNRATE < 9 and PAYEMS > 100k, or jobless claims rise by >10k.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising oil prices (not in catalog) would signal supply shocks, but with available data, declining INDPRO below 93.5 with rising FEDFUNDS above 0.15% indicates policy misalignment creating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 50%+ of periods when INDPRO < 93.5 and FEDFUNDS > 0.15, or real rates decline by >0.5pp.
